audience_description: Classroom teachers in Eduversal partner schools delivering Cambridge curriculum (Lower Secondary Checkpoint, IGCSE, AS/A-Level) alongside national curriculum. Year-1 teachers in induction overlay get extra tasks for first 12 weeks.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikbud 6/2018 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Defines teacher duty under principal supervision including planning, instruction, and assessment.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 13/2025 — Pendekatan Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Deep learning approach, 8 dimensi profil lulusan.

regulatory_anchors.title: PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — Standar Nasional Pendidikan
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Standar Isi, Standar Proses, Standar Penilaian apply to teacher practice.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge International Handbook for Centres
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Cambridge subject delivery standards and syllabus compliance.

framework_anchors.cambridge_teacher_standards_2023: Used for D1-D6 task mapping; tasks tagged with CTS reference where applicable.
framework_anchors.appraisal_framework_v2_1: Tasks accumulate evidence for F1 Administration, F2 Class Observation, F3 Work Inspection, F4 Student Survey.
framework_anchors.kpi_framework: teacher_kpi_config — tasks linked via competency_link feed teacher self-assessment

priority_levels.compliance: Legally required by Indonesian regulation OR contractually required by Cambridge/Eduversal. Non-negotiable.
priority_levels.core: Essential to effective performance. Strongly expected.
priority_levels.recommended: Best practice. Adopt where school context allows.

recurring_tasks.title: Submit lesson plans for the coming week
recurring_tasks.description: Submit complete lesson plans for every period you teach next week, using the Eduversal Lesson Plan Template (5 non-negotiable components: WALT, WILT, Differentiation, Instructional Sequence, Resource List).
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: Friday by 16:00 of the preceding week
evidence_required: Lesson plans submitted via school LMS
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Open the school LMS Lesson Plan module. Use the WALT/WILT framework — WALT describes what students will LEARN (not what they will DO). Reference your subject's scheme of work for the topic. If unsure, ask your HoD for an Exemplary plan from the Expert Directory.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Three consecutive late submissions trigger a coaching conversation with your HoD. Sustained pattern is escalated to the Academic Coordinator and noted in your appraisal F1 (Administration).

recurring_tasks.title: Update post-lesson reflection for every lesson delivered
recurring_tasks.description: After every lesson, add a 2-sentence reflection to the lesson plan: (1) what worked and why, (2) what you will change next time based on student responses.
evidence_required: Reflection field completed in LMS for every delivered lesson
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Don't write 'lesson went well'. Write what you saw: 'Students struggled with the second example because they didn't have the prerequisite vocab — next time pre-teach the 4 key terms in the starter.'
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: HoD spot-check (Section 6 Lesson Planning Quality Assurance) flags missing reflections. Pattern leads to Domain 6 development goal in IPDL.

recurring_tasks.title: Implement your Monday Morning Commitment from last PLC
recurring_tasks.description: The specific instructional change you committed to at the most recent Week-4 Strategy PLC. Your HoD will look for it in walkthroughs this week.
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: Throughout the week — visible in every lesson where applicable
evidence_required: Commitment visibly enacted in classroom practice
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Your PLC Monday Commitment is recorded by your HoD. It must be specific, measurable, and observable on Monday. If you forget what it was, ask your HoD or check the PLC minutes.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Commitment not visible in HoD walkthrough triggers a coaching conversation: 'What did you try? What did you notice?'

recurring_tasks.title: Mark and return one piece of substantive student work this week
recurring_tasks.description: At least one piece of meaningful work per class returned with specific written feedback that includes a named 'Next Step' for each student.
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: By Friday of the week the work was produced
evidence_required: Marked work returned to students with Next Step feedback
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: A grade alone is NOT feedback. Feedback tells the student what to DO next: 'Your conclusion needs to reference Source B — rewrite the final paragraph using PEEL structure.' If you have 30 students, you don't have to mark 30 essays this week — pick one piece of work from each class.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: HoD work-review (monthly) flags absent or generic feedback. Domain 5 is the most common appraisal weakness — sustained gaps trigger a Domain 5 coaching cycle.

recurring_tasks.title: Conduct at least 2 formative checks for understanding per lesson
recurring_tasks.description: Use techniques such as mini-whiteboards, exit tickets, cold-calling, think-pair-share, or hinge questions — at least twice per lesson — and visibly adjust instruction based on what students show.
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: A 'thumbs up if you understand' is NOT a check for understanding (everyone fakes it). Use techniques that produce evidence: 'Show me on your whiteboard the answer to Q3' is a check. Then ACT on what you see.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Walkthrough notes flag absent CFU. Becomes Monday Morning Commitment focus at next PLC.

recurring_tasks.title: Update your Individual Professional Development Log (IPDL)
recurring_tasks.description: Log any PD activity completed this week (peer observation, professional reading, workshop, PLC reflection). Add an Impact Statement: what changed in your practice as a result.
evidence_required: IPDL entry with activity + impact statement
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Your IPDL is a living record, not a HR form. Even 'I read 3 pages of Wiliam's Embedded Formative Assessment and tried the No Hands Up technique with Year 9' is a valid entry IF you note the impact: 'engagement noticeably higher; will continue Tuesday'.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Termly HoD review of IPDLs. A blank IPDL at term-end is a Domain 6 governance flag.

recurring_tasks.title: Complete attendance and grade entries on time
recurring_tasks.description: All daily attendance recorded by end of each day. Any assessed work graded and entered in the LMS within the deadline set by your HoD.
recurring_tasks.specific_timing: Daily for attendance; per assessment cycle for grades
evidence_required: Grade book current per HoD-stated deadline
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Attendance late = student safety risk (parents not notified of absence). Grade entry late = report card pipeline broken. Treat both as non-negotiable daily/weekly habits.
recurring_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Compliance failures appear in F1 Administration appraisal. Sustained pattern (3+ instances/term) is a written warning.

fortnightly_tasks.title: Receive your fortnightly walkthrough feedback
fortnightly_tasks.description: Your HoD or Academic Coordinator will conduct a 5-10 minute classroom walkthrough this fortnight (every teacher is visited at least once every 14 days). Read the 'I noticed / I wonder' feedback note within 24 hours of receiving it. Reflect on the 'I wonder' question.
fortnightly_tasks.specific_timing: After receiving feedback note (within 24 hours of HoD visit)
evidence_required: Reflection added to IPDL if substantial
fortnightly_tasks.first_time_guide: Walkthroughs are NOT appraisal. Your HoD is not scoring you — they are noticing patterns. The 'I wonder' question is a genuine invitation to reflect. Engage with it. The teachers who grow fastest are the ones who treat these as gold.
fortnightly_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Repeatedly ignoring walkthrough feedback is itself a pattern observed by your HoD. Becomes a Domain 6 development goal.

monthly_tasks.title: Attend the Week-4 Strategy PLC
monthly_tasks.description: 90-minute mandatory department PLC. Bring one piece of student work for the Artifact Analysis. Leave with a written Monday Morning Commitment.
monthly_tasks.specific_timing: Week 4 of every month, time set by HoD
evidence_required: Monday Morning Commitment written and recorded by HoD
monthly_tasks.first_time_guide: PLC is NOT a department meeting. 70% of the time is instructional dialogue. Bring real student work — not a hypothetical example. The Artifact Analysis follows Observe → Evaluate → Infer → Act. Your Monday Commitment must be specific: 'I will use 5-second wait time after every question on Monday and Tuesday' — not 'I will improve my questioning'.
monthly_tasks.missed_threshold_action: Three unexcused PLC absences in a year is a Domain 6 governance concern, escalated to the Academic Coordinator.

monthly_tasks.title: Submit one student work sample to PLC for collective review
monthly_tasks.description: Once per month, choose a piece of borderline student work (neither clearly excellent nor clearly failing) for the PLC's Artifact Analysis. Anonymise before submission.
monthly_tasks.specific_timing: Submitted to HoD 24 hours before Week-4 PLC
evidence_required: Anonymised work sample submitted to HoD
monthly_tasks.first_time_guide: Borderline work generates the richest conversation. Don't submit your best student's perfect essay. Submit the one that made you go 'hmm, why is this happening?' — that's the one your team can help you with.

termly_tasks.title: Complete termly self-assessment against the 6 Domains
termly_tasks.description: Twice a year (end of Sem-1, end of Sem-2), self-rate against the 6 Domains of Teaching Quality and write a reflective narrative. Discussed in HoD termly review.
termly_tasks.first_time_guide: Be honest. Rating yourself Exemplary on every domain when you've been teaching for 6 months is a Domain 6 weakness, not a Domain 6 strength. Your IPDL evidence should justify the level you claim.

1.phase: August — Tahun ajaran baru begins; MPLS
1.focus: Establishing routines, learning student names, baseline diagnostic prep

tasks.title: Establish 3 non-negotiable classroom routines before any instruction begins
tasks.description: Define and rehearse: (1) entry routine (what students do in the first 90 seconds), (2) transition signal (how you move from one task to the next), (3) attention signal (how you reclaim full attention).
tasks.first_time_guide: Domain 4 is the precondition for all other domains. A teacher with weak routines cannot deliver effective instruction in November either. Spend the time now.

tasks.title: Learn the names of every student in every class you teach
tasks.description: By the end of Week 1, you should be able to greet every student by name without prompting.
tasks.first_time_guide: This is a relationship investment that pays back every day for the rest of the year. Use a seating plan with photos. Practise nightly.

tasks.title: Read the Eduversal Academic Honesty Policy and AI Usage Policy
tasks.description: Familiarise yourself with the school's standards for student academic honesty, including AI tool usage boundaries.
tasks.first_time_guide: Get this clear before students hand in their first piece of homework. The most common AI-honesty disputes happen when teachers haven't communicated the boundary at the start of the year.

2.focus: Diagnostic assessment to establish starting points for each class

tasks.title: Administer baseline diagnostic for each class you teach
tasks.description: A short, low-stakes diagnostic that reveals each student's starting point in your subject. NOT a graded test — used to inform differentiation planning.
evidence_required: Diagnostic results recorded in LMS or grade book
tasks.first_time_guide: Your scheme of work assumes prior learning. Do not assume — verify. The baseline tells you which 3-4 students will need scaffolding from Week 3 onwards, and which 2-3 will need extension. Without this you'll be teaching to the middle for 40 weeks.

4.focus: First Domain Heatmap; first Monday Morning Commitment

tasks.title: Bring evidence from your first 3 weeks to the Strategy PLC
tasks.description: First PLC of the year — bring student work, lesson reflections, and at least one challenge you've encountered.
tasks.first_time_guide: First PLC sets the tone. Show up prepared. The teachers who treat PLC as a tickbox don't grow; the ones who bring real evidence do.

tasks.title: Verify your Cambridge candidate entries (if you teach IGCSE/AS/A-Level)
tasks.description: Confirm with the Cambridge Coordinator that all your students who should be entered for the Oct/Nov series have been entered correctly.
tasks.applies_when: subject is IGCSE / AS / A-Level Cambridge
tasks.first_time_guide: Wrong entries = student doesn't sit the paper they prepared for, OR sits the wrong paper. This is the single most consequential admin task in your year. Double-check the syllabus code, the option, and the tier.

tasks.title: Run final exam preparation review for Cambridge candidates
tasks.description: If you teach a Cambridge subject with Oct/Nov candidates: run final review using past papers, mark scheme analysis, and command-word familiarisation.
tasks.applies_when: subject is IGCSE / AS / A-Level Cambridge with Oct/Nov candidates
tasks.first_time_guide: Mark schemes are public. Use them. Show students how examiners read for specific command words ('describe' vs 'explain' vs 'evaluate' produce different mark allocations).

tasks.title: Prepare your students for EASE I
tasks.description: Familiarise students with EASE format and timing. EASE is network-wide and benchmarks every Eduversal partner school against each other.
tasks.first_time_guide: EASE results inform the next School Appraisal v2 visit. Your school's d4 (Achievement) rating is partially derived from EASE. Take it seriously.

tasks.title: Administer EASE I per the network protocol
tasks.description: Follow the timing, conditions, and submission protocol exactly as specified by the Cambridge Coordinator and Academic Coordinator.

16.focus: Internal moderation of Sem-1 summative assessments

tasks.title: Participate in cross-marking moderation session
tasks.description: Mark a sample of student work blind alongside colleagues. Resolve discrepancies against the rubric.
tasks.first_time_guide: If your blind-marking diverges from a colleague's by more than 2 grade boundaries on the same script, that's the conversation worth having. Don't defend your grade — investigate the divergence.

17.focus: Report card writing and termly self-assessment

tasks.title: Write Sem-1 report card comments for every student
tasks.description: Each comment must reference: (1) specific evidence of progress, (2) the named next step. No generic 'good progress' comments.
tasks.first_time_guide: Parents read the comment, not the grade. A specific comment ('Ahmad's algebraic manipulation has progressed; his next step is applying these skills to worded problems — he should attempt 5 problems weekly using the practice booklet') is worth more than a generic 'great work, keep it up'.

tasks.title: Archive Sem-1 lesson materials and student work samples
tasks.description: Organise lesson plans, marked work samples, and reflection notes for IPDL evidence and next-year planning.
evidence_required: Sem-1 archive folder organised in LMS
tasks.first_time_guide: Your Sem-1 lesson plans are gold for next year's planning. Archive them now while details are fresh. Future you will thank present you.

19.focus: Reset routines, set Sem-2 instructional priorities

tasks.title: Reset classroom routines and re-state expectations
tasks.description: After 2-3 weeks away, students need explicit re-grounding in classroom routines. Treat the first lesson back like Week 1.
tasks.first_time_guide: Don't assume routines hold across the break. Restate. Rehearse. Move on.

tasks.title: Set 1-2 new IPDL goals for Sem-2 based on Sem-1 evidence
tasks.description: Review your Sem-1 self-assessment with your HoD. Set 1-2 new outcome-based goals for the second half of the year.

25.focus: Cambridge candidate entries for May/June series

tasks.title: Verify your Cambridge May/June candidate entries
tasks.description: Confirm with Cambridge Coordinator: all your students entered for the correct papers, options, and tiers.
tasks.applies_when: subject is IGCSE / AS / A-Level Cambridge

tasks.title: Final exam-period instructional priorities
tasks.description: Cambridge May/June series begins. Final review for candidates. For non-candidate classes, maintain instructional rhythm — do NOT let exam fever displace teaching for non-candidates.
evidence_required: Lesson plans reflect appropriate exam-period adjustments

tasks.title: Administer EASE II per the network protocol
tasks.description: Follow the timing, conditions, and submission protocol exactly as specified by the Cambridge Coordinator and Academic Coordinator.

tasks.title: Administer end-of-Sem-2 summative assessments
tasks.description: Sem-2 final assessments per the school assessment calendar. Maintain academic honesty protocols.

38.focus: Year-end reflection and appraisal evidence finalisation

tasks.title: Complete final self-assessment for the year
tasks.description: Final 6-Domain self-assessment. Compile portfolio of evidence: signed IIPs, IPDL entries, walkthrough notes, peer observation reflections, student outcome data.
tasks.first_time_guide: Your portfolio is the evidence for your annual appraisal. The teacher who arrives with a well-organised portfolio shifts the appraisal from a judgement event to a reflective conversation. Build it as you go — don't assemble it the night before.

tasks.title: Attend your Annual Performance Appraisal meeting
tasks.description: Your appraiser will share F1-F4 evidence and the composite score. You speak first about your reflection, then receive evidence, then collaboratively set next-year goals.
evidence_required: Acknowledged or appealed within 5 working days
tasks.first_time_guide: If you disagree with a domain rating, you have the right to dispute (Section 5.11 of the Academic Leadership Governance Handbook). Read your IIP carefully before signing. Acknowledged ≠ agreed.

tasks.title: Archive year materials and prepare handover notes
tasks.description: Organise the full year's lesson materials. If a class is moving to a different teacher next year, prepare handover notes (cohort strengths, ongoing concerns, students requiring continued support).

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Fortnightly mentor meeting
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: Meet with your assigned mentor for 30 minutes every fortnight. Bring a specific question or concern. Mentor relationship is governed by the Mentor-Mentee Contract.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Monthly induction observation by mentor
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: Your mentor will observe one of your lessons each month (non-evaluative). You speak first in the post-observation reflection.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Weekly induction journal entry
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: Brief 3-sentence reflection: What went well? What was hard? What do I want to try next week?

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Weekly mood pulse
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: 1-5 score on how the week felt. Triggers a check-in if 2 consecutive weeks score ≤2.

provenance.regulatory_sources: Permendikbud 6/2018, Permendikdasmen 11/2025, 13/2025, PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — verbatim text in docs/research/permendiknas/
provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023, Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1, Eduversal Teacher Competency Framework (track: teachers)
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Adapted from Semesta School Academic Leadership Governance Handbook 2026-2027 with Eduversal-network adjustments (network-default + per-school override pattern)
